RESPONSE TO DEFT'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF KEANE, DON
REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOS. TO DEFT'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE PARTIES ENTERED INTO AN ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT BOTH PARTIES, TO DATE, HAVE FAILED TO COMPLY WITH ALL OF THE REQUIREMENTS TO COMMENCE THE ARBITRATION. HOWEVER, SUCH CONDUCT IS NOT A BASIS FOR CONCLUDING THAT THERE HAS BEEN A WAIVER, PARTICULARLY GIVEN THE LANGUAGE OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT REQUIRING THAT ANY WAIVER BE IN WRITING. THE POLICY OF THIS STATE FAVORS ARBITRATION WHERE THE PARTIES HAVE AGREED TO DO SO. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR# 7433

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2009 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2008 Continued To Jan-30-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ABSOLUTELYNEW, INC'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. It is undisputed that the parties entered into an enforceable arbitration agreement. It also appears that both parties, to date, have failed to comply with all of the requirements to commence the arbitration. However, such conduct is not a basis for concluding that there has been a waiver, particularly given the language of the parties' agreement requiring that any waiver be in writing. The policy of this state favors arbitration where the parties have agreed to do so. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AH-X) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Peppina Thompson, CSR# 7433
